description: Phase 6 periodization allocation engine for mealplan: implement deterministic carb redistribution with strict precedence, conflict handling, and reconciliation.

userStories.title: Implement domain-only periodization allocation API
userStories.description: As an orchestrator implementer, I want one pure domain allocation entrypoint so later orchestration can call periodization logic without duplicating rule code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add canonical API `calculate_periodized_carb_allocation(carb_mode: CarbMode, daily_carbs_g: float, training_before_meal: MealName | None, training_load_tomorrow: TrainingLoadTomorrow) -> dict[MealName, float]` in `src/mealplan/domain/services.py`.
API is domain-only with no application wrapper and no CLI wiring in Phase 6.
Output contract is canonical six-meal allocation keyed by `MealName` in canonical order.
Function signature is typed and deterministic for identical inputs.
Re-export `calculate_periodized_carb_allocation` from `src/mealplan/domain/__init__.py`.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement post-training two-high-meal rule
userStories.description: As a domain rule engine, I want the first two post-training meals marked high-carb so training timing drives carb concentration as specified.
acceptanceCriteria: Treat `training_before_meal` as the first post-training high-carb meal.
Treat the immediately next chronological canonical meal as the second high-carb meal.
Assign each high-carb meal exactly `0.30 * daily_carbs_g`.
Chronological next-meal logic handles end-of-day wrap deterministically in canonical order.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement low-meal residual distribution
userStories.description: As a domain rule engine, I want non-high meals to split remaining carbs evenly so total carbs are preserved and distribution is deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Compute remaining carbs as `daily_carbs_g - sum(high_meal_allocations)`.
Divide remaining carbs evenly across the four non-high meals.
Output includes all six canonical meals exactly once.
Allocation order matches canonical meal order.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement next-day high-load override with conflicts
userStories.description: As a domain rule engine, I want tomorrow-high override behavior applied with explicit conflict handling so precedence remains stable and predictable.
acceptanceCriteria: When `training_load_tomorrow == TrainingLoadTomorrow.HIGH`, apply `dinner` high and `evening-snack` low override.
Conflict condition is explicit: if `training_before_meal` is `MealName.DINNER` or `MealName.EVENING_SNACK`, preserve post-training selection and skip tomorrow-high override.
Handle all conflict and non-conflict branches explicitly and deterministically.
No implicit tie-breaking exists outside documented precedence.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Enforce precedence ordering explicitly
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want precedence encoded in one explicit sequence so future changes cannot silently reorder rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Keep rule execution order explicit: non-periodized bypass, post-training high rule, next-day high override unless conflict, reconciliation check.
Tests assert precedence outcomes, not only end totals.
Deterministic behavior is preserved for identical inputs.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Implement deterministic non-periodized bypass behavior
userStories.description: As a caller, I want a deterministic return shape when carb mode is not periodized so Phase 6 behavior is explicit before Phase 7 meal assembly.
acceptanceCriteria: For non-periodized modes, allocator bypasses redistribution rules.
Bypass returns deterministic equal split with each canonical meal set to `daily_carbs_g / 6.0` and no rounding in Phase 6.
Document bypass behavior as Phase 6 placeholder semantics for later Phase 7 integration.
Tests cover low and normal bypass cases with deterministic expected values.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Add tolerance-based carb reconciliation check
userStories.description: As a domain integrity guard, I want reconciliation validation with stable numeric tolerance so allocation totals remain correct without brittle float failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Add reconciliation check `abs(sum_allocated_carbs - daily_carbs_g) <= 1e-9`.
Reconciliation failure raises deterministic DomainRuleError.
Error category/message prefix is stable for testing and exit-code mapping.
Tests include pass and fail reconciliation scenarios.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Add exhaustive combinatorial precedence and conflict matrix tests
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want full combinatorial coverage across precedence dimensions so regressions in complex rule interactions are caught immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add exhaustive matrix over all training_before_meal values, all training_load_tomorrow states, and conflict/non-conflict branches.
Matrix asserts both meal-role selection and exact or tolerance-based totals.
Tests use deterministic expected outputs and stable case IDs.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.

userStories.title: Document Phase 6 allocator boundaries and contracts
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want clear contract documentation for periodization allocation so future phases integrate correctly without boundary leakage.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with Phase 6 technical boundary and precedence narrative.
Update docs/REQUIREMENTS.md with functional periodization and override behavior clarifications.
Update docs/MODEL.md with canonical domain contract details for allocator inputs/outputs, including `dict[MealName, float]` output shape.
Documentation states tolerance policy 1e-9 and deterministic non-periodized bypass behavior.
mypy --strict src passes.
pytest passes.
